mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly employment growth above 100k signals robust labor market conditions, increasing consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain negative in 70%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k monthly growth.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating GDP growth above 2% annualized indicates strong economic demand, boosting corporate profits and equity valuations, which predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns fail to exceed 1.5% in 60%+ of periods with GDPC1 growth above 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in GDP growth and housing starts signal synchronized demand expansion across sectors, creating positive momentum in equity markets that drives S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and HOUST both rise > 1% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where both GDPC1 and HOUST show month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production above 0.5% month-over-month indicates improving supply-side capacity utilization, reducing inflationary pressures and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 60%+ of periods with INDPRO growth above 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% annualized prompts central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of periods with CPIAUCSL above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: In high inflation regimes, rising federal funds rates above 4.5% signal aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 50%+ of instances where both CPIAUCSL > 3% and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in unemployment and rising employment gains indicate tightening labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 50%+ of instances where UNRATE declines and PAYEMS rises simultaneously.
